question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NewConstructionAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a service restart (e.g., via a crash) that causes NewConstructionAPIService() to call hiero.ClientForName(network) which makes a blocking network call, and if the network is unreachable, the service hangs indefinitely during initialization, preventing all transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NewConstructionAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that sdkClient.SetMaxAttempts(1) disables SDK auto-retry, so that a single transient network error during hiero.TransactionExecute() causes the transaction to fail permanently, and by flooding the network with such transient-error-inducing requests, prevent all legitimate transactions from being confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a signed transaction where transaction.GetNodeAccountIDs()[0] panics due to an empty node account IDs slice (since the transaction was constructed without a node ID), causing a nil/index panic in the log.Infof call and crashing the service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where hiero.TransactionGetTransactionID() returns an error (second return value ignored with '_'), causing transactionId to be a zero-value that is logged but the transaction is still executed, potentially causing the network to reject it due to an invalid transaction ID?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that hiero.TransactionGetTransactionID() errors are silently ignored (assigned to '_'), and craft a transaction where the transaction ID is zero/empty, causing the Hiero network to reject it with a INVALID_TRANSACTION_ID error, and by flooding with such transactions, exhaust network node resources?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: transactionSetNodeAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a node_account_id in metadata that corresponds to a valid account ID format but belongs to a non-consensus node, causing hiero.TransactionSetNodeAccountIDs() to succeed but the transaction to be routed to a node that cannot process it, effectively preventing confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getTransactionNodeAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a node_account_id metadata value that is a valid string but parses to a shard/realm/account combination that does not exist in the network, causing all transactions constructed with this metadata to be rejected by the network and preventing confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getTransactionNodeAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a node_account_id metadata value as a non-string type (e.g., a JSON integer), causing the type assertion value.(string) to fail and return ErrInvalidArgument, and by flooding the /construction/payloads endpoint with such requests, exhaust server resources and prevent legitimate transaction construction?
